
### Disclaimer — this list was generated by Claude

Here's a comprehensive list organized by module, with a focus on what would make the language feel complete and practical:

---

## `bio` (I/O)
- [x] `readLine()` — read a line from stdin
- [x] `readFile(path)` — read entire file as string
- [x] `writeFile(path, content)` — write string to file
- [x] `appendFile(path, content)` — append to file
- [ ] `fileExists(path)` — returns bool
- [ ] `deleteFile(path)` — delete a file
- [ ] `readDir(path)` — returns array of filenames in directory
- [ ] `makeDir(path)` — create directory

---

## `bstring` (Strings)
- [x] `length(s)` — string length
- [x] `split(s, delim)` — returns array of substrings
- [x] `join(arr, delim)` — joins array into string
- [x] `trim(s)` — strip whitespace from both ends
- [ ] `trimLeft(s)` / `trimRight(s)`
- [x] `toUpper(s)` / `toLower(s)`
- [ ] `startsWith(s, prefix)` / `endsWith(s, suffix)`
- [ ] `contains(s, sub)` — returns bool
- [ ] `replace(s, from, to)` — replace first occurrence
- [ ] `replaceAll(s, from, to)`
- [x] `indexOf(s, sub)` — returns index or -1
- [x] `substring(s, start, end)`
- [x] `charAt(s, i)` — returns character at index
- [ ] `repeat(s, n)` — repeat string n times
- [ ] `parseInt(s)` / `parseFloat(s)` — string to number
- [ ] `toString(x)` — number/bool to string

---

## `bmath` (Math)
- [x] `floor(x)` / `ceil(x)` / `round(x)`
- [ ] `abs(x)`
- [x] `sqrt(x)` / `cbrt(x)`
- [x] `pow(x, y)`
- [x] `log(x)` / `log2(x)` / `log10(x)`
- [x] `sin(x)` / `cos(x)` / `tan(x)`
- [x] `asin(x)` / `acos(x)` / `atan(x)` / `atan2(y, x)`
- [ ] `min(a, b)` / `max(a, b)`
- [ ] `clamp(x, lo, hi)`
- [ ] `sign(x)` — returns -1, 0, or 1
- [x] `random()` — float in [0, 1)
- [x] `randomInt(lo, hi)` — integer in [lo, hi]
- [x] `PI` / `E` / `INF` / `NAN` — constants

---

## `barray` (Arrays)
- [ ] `push(arr, val)` / `pop(arr)`
- [ ] `shift(arr)` / `unshift(arr, val)` — front operations
- [ ] `insert(arr, i, val)` / `remove(arr, i)`
- [ ] `slice(arr, start, end)`
- [ ] `concat(arr1, arr2)`
- [ ] `reverse(arr)`
- [ ] `sort(arr)` / `sortBy(arr, fn)` — comparator-based sort
- [ ] `find(arr, fn)` — first matching element
- [ ] `filter(arr, fn)` — returns new filtered array
- [ ] `map(arr, fn)` — returns new mapped array
- [ ] `reduce(arr, fn, init)`
- [ ] `forEach(arr, fn)`
- [ ] `contains(arr, val)` — returns bool
- [ ] `indexOf(arr, val)`
- [ ] `fill(arr, val)` — fill all slots with value
- [ ] `copy(arr)` — shallow copy

---

## `bcast` (Type Casting / Reflection)
- [x] `typeOf(x)` — returns string name of type
- [ ] `toNumber(x)` / `toBool(x)` / `toString(x)`

---

## `btime` (Time)
- [x] `clock()` — seconds since program start (already common)
- [x] `time()` — Unix timestamp in seconds
- [ ] `timeMs()` — Unix timestamp in milliseconds
- [x] `sleep(ms)` — pause execution
- [ ] `formatTime(timestamp, fmt)` — format a timestamp as string

---













## `bsys` (System) — new module
- `args()` — returns array of command-line arguments
- `env(name)` — get environment variable
- `exit(code)` — terminate with exit code
- `run(cmd)` — run a shell command, return output as string
- `platform()` — returns `"linux"`, `"windows"`, `"macos"` etc.

---

## `bnet` (Networking) — ambitious but impactful
- `httpGet(url)` — returns response body as string
- `httpPost(url, body)` — returns response body
- `httpGetJson(url)` — returns parsed JSON as nested arrays/values
- `socketOpen(host, port)` — returns a socket handle
- `socketSend(sock, data)`
- `socketReceive(sock)` — returns string
- `socketClose(sock)`

---

## `bjson` (JSON) — new module
- `jsonParse(s)` — parse JSON string into Burrito values
- `jsonStringify(x)` — serialize Burrito value to JSON string

---

## `bgraphics` (Graphics — already exists)
Presumably already has windowing/drawing. Worth adding if not present:
- `loadImage(path)` / `drawImage(img, x, y)`
- `drawText(s, x, y, size, color)`
- `getMousePos()` — returns array `[x, y]`
- `isKeyDown(key)` / `isKeyPressed(key)`
- `playSound(path)` / `stopSound()`

---

The highest-value additions for making the language feel real day-to-day would be **`bstring`**, **`barray`**, and **`bsys`** — those are what you reach for constantly in any practical script. `bjson` and `bnet` are what make it useful for real tools.
